title: Identity, Permissions, Ownership, umask, and ACLs · Terminal Trace: User Group Identity
summary: Create a shared operations directory that grants required access without world-writable shortcuts or unexplained privilege escalation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux user group identity increment in a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret real and effective user, primary and supplementary groups, and process credentials from id and process evidence.
Choose cut, paste, tr, sed, awk, or a structured parser based on delimiter, record, escaping, and mutation requirements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict User Group Identity
steps.instruction:
theory case — Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the User Group Identity contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for interpret real and effective user, primary and supplementary groups, and process credentials from id and process evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback. Apply that move specifically to User Group Identity while producing a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Directory /srv/reports is owned by learner:lab with mode 2770 and umask 0027. A lab-group writer and outside reader attempt create, list, delete, and execute operations; derive each result before changing ACLs. Competency probe: Interpret real and effective user, primary and supplementary groups, and process credentials from id and process evidence. A defensible solution applies User Group Identity, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a least-privilege access matrix and permission lab.

content.code:
# Worked focus: linux-user-group-identity — Interpret real and effective user, primary and supplementary groups, and process credentials from id and process evidence.
id
umask
stat -c 'mode=%a owner=%U group=%G path=%n' reports
# Derive directory create/delete behavior before any chmod or ACL change.
